nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q7.alt: Question 7. In paragraph 3, when the author says that "Neil felt like the universe was calling him," she is referring to how Choices: A: he heard the booming voice inside the Hayden Planetarium on his first visit B: he was delighted by seeing the stars inside the Hayden Planetarium C: his experiences looking at the stars made him want to learn more about astronomy D: he wanted to spend more time in the countryside because he could see more of the sky

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q8.alt: Question 8. Paragraph 6 of the passage supports paragraph 3 by showing that Neil Choices: A: saw the same things at home that he saw at the planetarium B: wanted to return to Pennsylvania to use his new binoculars C: continued his interest in learning about the universe D: tried to share his interest in stars with his parents

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q9.alt: Question 9. What do paragraphs 6 through 8 show about Neil's parents? Choices: A: They work hard to give Neil everything he wants. B: They are supportive of Neil's interests. C: They set good examples for Neil to follow. D: They want Neil to become a scientist.

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q10.alt: Question 10. The information in the section "LEARNING ABOUT THE UNIVERSE" adds to the information in the rest of the passage by showing how Neil Choices: A: finally became a successful student B: made new discoveries with his telescope C: made choices that helped him become an astrophysicist D: earned money to pay for his education as an astrophysicist

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q11.alt: Question 11. Which sentence best supports the main idea of the passage? Choices: A: "Not long after this, Neil and his family took a trip to Pennsylvania." (paragraph 3) B: "Neil lived with his parents, his older brother, and his younger sister in a tall building called the Skyview Apartments." (paragraph 4) C: "In fact, his third-grade teacher wrote a note on his report card." (paragraph 5) D: "He often took his telescope to the roof of his apartment to study the night sky." (paragraph 10)

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q12.alt: Question 12. According to the passage, which sentence best describes Neil? Choices: A: He is serious about reaching his goals. B: He is tired from working different jobs. C: He is proud of his success in school. D: He is happy to help his family earn money.

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q19.alt: Question 19. Which sentence best explains why Hayley is chosen to perform in the talent show? Choices: A: Other students think she is cool. B: She plays the ukulele well in the tryouts. C: Her act is more unusual than others'. D: Her great-great aunt used to play music.

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q20.alt: Question 20. Why are paragraphs 4 through 7 important to the story? Choices: A: They show how accepting Mr. Y is. B: They describe how confused Tilly is. C: They describe how worried Hayley's dad is. D: They show how supportive Hayley's family is.

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q21.alt: Question 21. Which statement about the talent show is true? Choices: A: Most students like Hayley's talent show act the best. B: The talent show has performers from different grades. C: Some performers canceled their acts in the talent show. D: Hayley knows all the other performers in the talent show.

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q22.alt: Question 22. Which paragraph does the illustration best support? Choices: A: paragraph 10 B: paragraph 11 C: paragraph 13 D: paragraph 15

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q23.alt: Question 23. Which happens as a result of Hayley's performance in the talent show? Choices: A: Many students respect Hayley's musical ability. B: Many students learn to play the ukulele. C: Skeeter starts a band for ukulele players. D: Hayley understands how to be a celebrity.

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q24.alt: Question 24. Which sentence best states a central message of the story? Choices: A: Learning to do something new takes time. B: Making mistakes is a part of growing up. C: Conquering fear can lead to success. D: Sharing with friends brings happiness.

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q25.alt: Question 25. What is Kathryn's first clue that Nitro is blind? Choices: A: Nitro seems to be afraid. B: Nitro does not know where his food is. C: Nitro runs into the fence. D: Nitro does not look outside his cage.

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q26.alt: Question 26. Which quotation best states the main idea of the passage? Choices: A: "'He would sit and stare at the concrete walls,' Kathryn said." (paragraph 3) B: "That cage had been so small, Nitro had been able to memorize every inch." (paragraph 5) C: "'We just don't know what caused his condition,' Kathryn said." (paragraph 7) D: "They had to help a blind tiger find his way, without the use of his eyes." (paragraph 8)

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q27.alt: Question 27. Which detail from the passage best shows that Kathryn cares about Nitro? Choices: A: She puts Nitro into a new cage. B: She does not want Nitro to hurt himself. C: She thinks Nitro has a great attitude. D: She does not understand why Nitro is blind.

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q28.alt: Question 28. Which statement from the passage shows that the problem in paragraph 8 was solved? Choices: A: "Nitro walked through his big new home." (paragraph 9) B: "He was trying to feel what was ahead of him." (paragraph 9) C: "Once he learned where his fences were, the people at the Rescue put down sand pathways." (paragraph 13) D: "In time, Nitro learned where every bump, every tree, and every food box was in his new cage." (paragraph 15)

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q29.alt: Question 29. In paragraph 16, what does the word "mastered" suggest about Nitro? Choices: A: that he needed a larger space B: that he felt comfortable in his space C: that he liked people to visit his space D: that he wanted to defend his space

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q30.alt: Question 30. What causes Nitro to chuffle? Choices: A: being aware of people B: feeling afraid C: wanting a treat D: having a cough

nysed-ela-2017-g3-mc-q31.alt: Question 31. What makes Nitro “a Rescue favorite” (paragraph 18)? Choices: A: his reaction to visitors B: his response to peppermint C: his unusual chuffle D: his large paws
